name: dev-loop
description: Eight autonomous agents — PM, QA, Dev, Sweep, Reflect, Ops, Architect, and Director — that run a software-development loop coordinated entirely through ticket state. Five are inward / build-facing; three are OUTWARD agents (conventions §21): Ops watches RUNNING prod over time and files Urgent `incident` Bugs on a confirmed+repeated degradation (anti-flap re-check + dedupe, never auto-rollback); Architect audits whole-codebase technical health on a rotating, SHA-gated dimension and files `tech-debt` Improvements (read-only, never implements); the Director owns DIRECTION — on the optional `service` hub it chairs a cross-agent DISCUSSION BOARD (opens topics, the role-lens agents post per-round perspectives, the Director synthesizes → a decision) and DRAFTS the kind:"roadmap" doc that the OPERATOR publishes, folding optional real-user `signalSources` (PII-safe; graceful no-op without a `director` config; conventions §25). Ops/Architect observe-and-file; the Director coordinates + drafts; none implements, ships, verifies, or auto-applies a structural change (§17 — a structural ask stays a `[director-proposal]` ticket). PM proposes and verifies features from a strategy doc; QA hunts and re-tests bugs; Dev pulls Todo tickets in priority order, implements, self-reviews, ships, and smoke-checks prod (auto-revert on a break); Sweep is a lifecycle janitor that fixes tickets falling through the cracks (missing/wrong owner labels, orphaned In Progress); Reflect is a daily retrospective that studies the loop's own behavior and curates the per-operator lessons.md from recurring evidence (drafting, never auto-applying, structural changes). A one-time `init` setup command (not a loop agent) bootstraps a product into the loop with a DETECT → MAP → ASSEMBLE → LOAD flow — it detects the project shape (greenfield / brownfield / adopting; single- or multi-repo), read-only-maps a brownfield codebase into a fixed-heading PM doc-base, runs a greenfield strategy interview when there is no code yet, gathers config (incl. extra repos), provisions Linear labels/project (and one `repo:<name>` label per repo when multi-repo), scaffolds the strategy doc/test env/runtime files, optionally adopts named pre-existing human tickets with per-ticket operator confirmation, and prints a readiness checklist. The loop coordinates one or many repos: tickets target a repo via a `repo:<name>` label, with per-repo build/branch/deploy resolution (single-repo stays 100% unchanged). Every ticket the system touches carries a `dev-loop` label so it never disturbs the human backlog. Coordination runs through a configurable backend — Linear (default) or a machine-local file board (`backend:"local"`) with the same state machine and protocols. Every agent also writes daily/weekly/monthly reports to the data dir and acts on any operator review (点评) of them — distilling the critique into a per-agent lessons.md rule that improves its working method (conventions §22). Reports default to machine-local files; an opt-in reports.sink:"linear" (conventions §23) instead hosts the report body and the 点评 channel in Linear for cloud/remote runtimes (default-off, with §16 guardrails and channel-split review provenance). An OPTIONAL Codex companion (conventions §24, via the codex-plugin-cc plugin + the codex CLI; absent OR disabled OR no codex on PATH ⇒ 100% unchanged) gives the loop an advisory second-model review (Dev Step 5.5 + Architect), native image generation (PM mockups + Dev production assets — the one capability the agents lack), and a one-shot rescue before a fix-exhausted block — all gated per sub-flag, advisory only, and never touching Linear. When a ticket is left human-parked for the operator (blocked+needs-pm+external-prereq), PM pings you out-of-band via an optional Slack/Lark webhook (conventions §9 `notify`; default-off, announced once, secret-safe). The coordination backend is pluggable (conventions §18): Linear (default), a machine-local file board, or — new — a local `service` hub (an MCP system-of-record over node:sqlite, zero native deps; see docs/HUB-ARCHITECTURE.md) that gives the loop real PER-AGENT IDENTITY (every write attributable, not Linear's single shared user), versioned operator-published DOCUMENTS, the §25 DISCUSSION BOARD + Director, an optional poll-based two-way IM CHANNEL (Lark/Slack — the operator chats with the Director; §16-secret-safe, no daemon), and an optional ONE-WAY Linear MIRROR (the hub pushes its tickets to Linear for human visibility; split-brain enforced — Linear is never read back as truth), and is CLI-PORTABLE (the same agents + hub run on a second coding CLI — Codex/opencode — against the same db, gated on a per-CLI identity-propagation test; docs/PORTABILITY.md); opt-in, Linear + Claude Code stay the default.
